danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprite design and palette differentiation from Planeteers
danger_signals.meaning: Identifies antagonistic forces threatening environmental safety

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual environmental degradation, toxic sprites, hazardous terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Communicates active threats to player progression and world state

danger_signals.visual_form: UI bar reduction, character visual feedback
danger_signals.meaning: Signals immediate danger to player character survival

safety_signals.visual_form: Friendly character sprites with distinct color coding
safety_signals.meaning: Identifies allied forces and safe interaction points

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual clearing of pollution, brightened palette areas
safety_signals.meaning: Communicates successful objective completion and world improvement

safety_signals.visual_form: Character visual enhancement, special effects display
safety_signals.meaning: Indicates temporary invulnerability or enhanced capabilities

faction_indicators.color_identity: Varied per character (Earth/Green, Fire/Red, Wind/Blue, Water/Cyan, Heart/Pink)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual Planeteer rings (Earth, Fire, Wind, Water, Heart rings)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Clean, restored environments represent Planeteer victory zones

faction_indicators.color_identity: Darker, more sinister palette than heroes
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual villain identity through sprite design and behavior patterns
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each eco-villain has distinct visual presentation reflecting their pollution theme
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Polluted, degraded environments with toxic visual indicators mark villain influence zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Darkened color palette, toxic sprite overlays, hazardous terrain visual indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Eco-villains present and active, hazards spawn or persist in affected areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Brightened palette, removal of toxic visual elements, environmental restoration indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Eco-villains defeated or removed from stage, hazards disappear

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Planeteer power active (Captain Planet summoned or ring power used)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enhanced character sprite display, special effects, color intensification
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased movement speed, damage output, or invulnerability period

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character sprite flashing or visual damage indicators, diminished UI bars
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced effectiveness, slower movement, increased vulnerability to hazards

environmental_storytelling.example: Toxic clouds, darkened water, damaged vegetation indicate eco-villain activity and environmental degradation severity
Each Planeteer and their ring color reflects their elemental power (green=earth, red=fire, blue=wind, cyan=water, pink=heart)
Eco-villain character designs visually distinguish them from heroic Planeteers through silhouette and palette choice
Player sees visual world state improvement as pollution is cleared, creating feedback loop for objective completion

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard movement, attack patterns, environmental interaction capabilities
Reduced mobility, visual sprite flickering or damage indicators
Active pollution generation, environmental hazard spawning, hostile movement patterns
Removal from stage, environmental pollution cessation, visual clearing of contamination
Enhanced combat capability, visual special effects, temporary invulnerability or power boost display

notes: Source material is limited to development/publication metadata and unused music notation. Game-specific manifestation patterns (danger signals, faction indicators, world-state visuals, behavioral tells) are inferred from game IP canon (Captain Planet animated series visual language) and typical NES platformer/action game design conventions of the era. Direct gameplay footage or detailed design documentation not available in provided source. Tier T3 qualitative assessment appropriate given data limitations.
sources_used: tcrf - Captain Planet and the Planeteers (NES) - The Cutting Room Floor
